core.Sign of Cross: In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.
core.Sign of Cross LA: In nomine Patris, et Filii, et Spiritus Sancti. Amen.
core.Apostles Creed:
I believe in God, the Father Almighty, Creator of Heaven and earth; and in Jesus Christ, His only Son, Our Lord, Who was conceived by the Holy Spirit, born of the Virgin Mary, suffered under Pontius Pilate, was crucified, died, and was buried.

He descended into hell; on the third day He arose again from the dead; He ascended into Heaven, and is seated at the right hand of God, the Father Almighty; from thence He shall come to judge the living and the dead.

I believe in the Holy Spirit, the holy Catholic Church, the communion of saints, the forgiveness of sins, the resurrection of the body, and life everlasting. Amen.
core.Apostles Creed LA:
Credo in Deum Patrem omnipotentem, Creatorem caeli et terrae. Et in Iesum Christum, Filium eius unicum, Dominum nostrum, qui conceptus est de Spiritu Sancto, natus ex Maria Virgine, passus sub Pontio Pilato, crucifixus, mortuus, et sepultus.

Descendit ad inferos; tertia die resurrexit a mortuis; ascendit ad caelos; sedet ad dexteram Dei Patris omnipotentis; inde venturus est iudicare vivos et mortuos.

Credo in Spiritum Sanctum, sanctam Ecclesiam catholicam, sanctorum communionem, remissionem peccatorum, carnis resurrectionem, vitam aeternam. Amen.
core.Our Father:
Our Father, Who art in heaven, hallowed be Thy name; Thy kingdom come; Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven.

Give us this day our daily bread; and forgive us our trespasses as we forgive those who trespass against us; and lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil. Amen.
core.Our Father LA:
Pater noster, qui es in caelis, sanctificetur nomen tuum. Adveniat regnum tuum. Fiat voluntas tua, sicut in caelo et in terra.

Panem nostrum quotidianum da nobis hodie, et dimitte nobis debita nostra sicut et nos dimittimus debitoribus nostris. Et ne nos inducas in tentationem, sed libera nos a malo. Amen.
core.Hail Mary:
Hail Mary, full of grace. The Lord is with thee. Blessed art thou amongst women, and blessed is the fruit of thy womb, Jesus.

Holy Mary, Mother of God, pray for us sinners, now and at the hour of our death. Amen.
core.Hail Mary LA:
Ave Maria, gratia plena, Dominus tecum. Benedicta tu in mulieribus, et benedictus fructus ventris tui, Iesus.

Sancta Maria, Mater Dei, ora pro nobis peccatoribus, nunc et in hora mortis nostrae. Amen.
core.Glory Be:
Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit.

As it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world without end. Amen.
core.Glory Be LA:
Gloria Patri, et Filio, et Spiritui Sancto.

Sicut erat in principio, et nunc, et semper, et in saecula saeculorum. Amen.
core.Fatima Prayer: O my Jesus, forgive us our sins, save us from the fires of hell, and lead all souls to Heaven, especially those in most need of Thy mercy.
core.Fatima Prayer LA: O mi Iesu, dimitte nobis debita nostra, salva nos ab igne inferni, perduc in caelum omnes animas, praesertim eas quae maxime indigent misericordia tua.
core.Hail Holy Queen:
Hail, Holy Queen, Mother of Mercy, our life, our sweetness and our hope.

To thee do we cry, poor banished children of Eve; to thee do we send up our sighs, mourning and weeping in this valley of tears.

Turn then, most gracious Advocate, thine eyes of mercy toward us, and after this our exile, show unto us the blessed fruit of thy womb, Jesus.

O clement, O loving, O sweet Virgin Mary!

Pray for us, O Holy Mother of God, that we may be made worthy of the promises of Christ.
core.Hail Holy Queen LA:
Salve, Regina, Mater misericordiae; vita, dulcedo et spes nostra, salve.

Ad te clamamus exsules filii Hevae. Ad te suspiramus gementes et flentes in hac lacrimarum valle.

Eia ergo, advocata nostra, illos tuos misericordes oculos ad nos converte. Et Iesum, benedictum fructum ventris tui, nobis post hoc exsilium ostende.

O clemens, O pia, O dulcis Virgo Maria!

Ora pro nobis, Sancta Dei Genitrix, ut digni efficiamur promissionibus Christi.
core.Memorare:
Remember, O most gracious Virgin Mary, that never was it known that anyone who fled to thy protection, implored thy help, or sought thy intercession was left unaided.

Inspired by this confidence, I fly unto thee, O Virgin of virgins, my Mother; to thee do I come, before thee I stand, sinful and sorrowful.

O Mother of the Word Incarnate, despise not my petitions, but in thy mercy hear and answer me. Amen.
core.Memorare LA:
Memorare, O piissima Virgo Maria, non esse auditum a saeculo, quemquam ad tua currentem praesidia, tua implorantem auxilia, tua petentem suffragia, esse derelictum.

Ego tali animatus confidentia ad te, Virgo Virginum, Mater, curro, ad te venio, coram te gemens peccator assisto.

Noli, Mater Verbi, verba mea despicere; sed audi propitia et exaudi. Amen.
core.Rosary Closing:
O God, whose only begotten Son, by His life, death, and resurrection, has purchased for us the rewards of eternal life, grant we beseech Thee, that meditating upon these mysteries of the Most Holy Rosary of the Blessed Virgin Mary, we may imitate what they contain and obtain what they promise, through the same Christ Our Lord. Amen.

In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.
core.Rosary Closing LA:
Deus, cuius Unigenitus per vitam, mortem et resurrectionem suam nobis salutis aeternae praemia comparavit, concede, quaesumus, ut haec mysteria sacratissimo beatae Mariae Virginis Rosario recolentes et imitemur quod continent, et quod promittunt assequamur. Per eundem Christum Dominum nostrum. Amen.

In nomine Patris, et Filii, et Spiritus Sancti. Amen.

Joyful.title: 1st Joyful Mystery — The Annunciation
Joyful.verse: Luke 1:28 — "Hail, full of grace, the Lord is with you!"
Joyful.meditation: The Angel Gabriel announces to the Virgin Mary that she is to be the Mother of God. Mary humbly submits to God's will: 'Behold the handmaid of the Lord; be it done to me according to thy word.' Let us ask for the virtue of humility.

Joyful.title: 2nd Joyful Mystery — The Visitation
Joyful.verse: Luke 1:42 — "Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb!"
Joyful.meditation: Mary visits her cousin Elizabeth, who is with child. At Mary's greeting, John leaps for joy in the womb, and Elizabeth cries: 'Blessed are you among women!' Let us ask for the virtue of charity toward our neighbors.

Joyful.title: 3rd Joyful Mystery — The Nativity
Joyful.verse: Luke 2:11 — "For to you is born this day in the city of David a Saviour, who is Christ the Lord."
Joyful.meditation: Jesus Christ, the Son of God, is born in a stable in Bethlehem. Angels announce His birth to the shepherds, and the star guides the Magi to the newborn King. Let us ask for the virtue of poverty of spirit and detachment from worldly things.

Joyful.title: 4th Joyful Mystery — The Presentation
Joyful.verse: Luke 2:30–31 — "Mine eyes have seen thy salvation which thou hast prepared in the presence of all peoples."
Joyful.meditation: Mary and Joseph present the child Jesus in the Temple. Simeon, filled with the Holy Spirit, takes Jesus in his arms and declares Him the Light of the World. Let us ask for the virtue of obedience to God's law.

Joyful.title: 5th Joyful Mystery — Finding in the Temple
Joyful.verse: Luke 2:49 — "Did you not know that I must be in my Father's house?"
Joyful.meditation: After three days of searching, Mary and Joseph find the twelve-year-old Jesus in the Temple, sitting among the teachers, listening and asking questions. Let us ask for the virtue of zeal for the things of God.

Sorrowful.title: 1st Sorrowful Mystery — Agony in the Garden
Sorrowful.verse: Luke 22:42 — "Father, if thou art willing, remove this cup from me; nevertheless not my will, but thine, be done."
Sorrowful.meditation: Jesus prays in the Garden of Gethsemane until His sweat becomes as drops of blood. He accepts the Father's will: 'Not my will, but Thine be done.' Let us ask for the virtue of contrition for our sins.

Sorrowful.title: 2nd Sorrowful Mystery — Scourging at the Pillar
Sorrowful.verse: Isaiah 53:5 — "He was wounded for our transgressions, he was bruised for our iniquities."
Sorrowful.meditation: Jesus is bound to a pillar and scourged mercilessly. He bears every blow in reparation for the sins of the flesh and in love for each soul. Let us ask for the virtue of purity and mortification.

Sorrowful.title: 3rd Sorrowful Mystery — Crowning with Thorns
Sorrowful.meditation: Soldiers weave a crown of thorns and press it upon Jesus' head, mocking Him as King of the Jews. He receives it in silence, a King of suffering and love. Let us ask for the virtue of courage and moral fortitude.

Sorrowful.title: 4th Sorrowful Mystery — Carrying of the Cross
Sorrowful.verse: Luke 23:26 — "As they led him away, they seized one Simon of Cyrene... and laid on him the cross, to carry it behind Jesus."
Sorrowful.meditation: Jesus carries His heavy Cross through the streets of Jerusalem to Calvary, falling three times under its weight, yet rising each time to continue. Let us ask for the virtue of patience in our own sufferings.

Sorrowful.title: 5th Sorrowful Mystery — The Crucifixion
Sorrowful.verse: John 19:30 — "It is finished." And he bowed his head and gave up his spirit.
Sorrowful.meditation: Jesus is nailed to the Cross and dies after three hours of agony. His final words are a prayer of forgiveness and total surrender to the Father. Let us ask for the grace of final perseverance.

Glorious.title: 1st Glorious Mystery — The Resurrection
Glorious.verse: John 20:17 — "I am ascending to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God."
Glorious.meditation: On the third day, Jesus rises from the dead in glory. He appears to Mary Magdalene, the disciples on the road to Emmaus, and the Apostles, confirming eternal life. Let us ask for the virtue of faith.

Glorious.title: 2nd Glorious Mystery — The Ascension
Glorious.verse: Acts 1:11 — "This Jesus, who was taken up from you into heaven, will come in the same way as you saw him go."
Glorious.meditation: Forty days after the Resurrection, Jesus ascends bodily into Heaven before His disciples on the Mount of Olives, promising the gift of the Holy Spirit. Let us ask for the virtue of hope and desire for heaven.

Glorious.title: 3rd Glorious Mystery — Descent of the Holy Spirit
Glorious.verse: Acts 2:4 — "They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other tongues."
Glorious.meditation: The Holy Spirit descends upon the Apostles and Mary in the Upper Room as tongues of fire on Pentecost, empowering them to proclaim the Gospel to all nations. Let us ask for the gifts of the Holy Spirit.

Glorious.title: 4th Glorious Mystery — The Assumption
Glorious.verse: Revelation 12:1 — "A great portent appeared in heaven, a woman clothed with the sun, with the moon under her feet."
Glorious.meditation: At the end of her earthly life, the Virgin Mary is taken body and soul into heavenly glory, a foretaste of the resurrection of all the faithful. Let us ask for the grace of a holy death.

Glorious.title: 5th Glorious Mystery — Coronation of Mary
Glorious.verse: Revelation 12:1 — "On her head a crown of twelve stars."
Glorious.meditation: Mary is crowned Queen of Heaven and Earth, Queen of Angels and Saints, interceding for us before the throne of her Son for all eternity. Let us ask for perseverance in devotion to Mary.

Luminous.title: 1st Luminous Mystery — Baptism in the Jordan
Luminous.verse: Matthew 3:17 — "This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased."
Luminous.meditation: Jesus is baptized by John in the Jordan River. The heavens open, the Spirit descends as a dove, and the Father proclaims: 'This is my beloved Son.' Let us ask for openness to the Holy Spirit.

Luminous.title: 2nd Luminous Mystery — Wedding at Cana
Luminous.verse: John 2:5 — "His mother said to the servants, 'Do whatever he tells you.'"
Luminous.meditation: At Mary's intercession, Jesus performs His first miracle at the wedding in Cana, turning water into wine and revealing His glory to His disciples. Let us ask for a deeper trust in Mary's intercession.

Luminous.title: 3rd Luminous Mystery — Proclamation of the Kingdom
Luminous.verse: Mark 1:15 — "The kingdom of God is at hand; repent, and believe in the gospel."
Luminous.meditation: Jesus proclaims the Kingdom of God, calling all to conversion of heart, and confirms His divine message with signs and miracles. Let us ask for the virtue of conversion and penance.

Luminous.title: 4th Luminous Mystery — The Transfiguration
Luminous.verse: Matthew 17:5 — "This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased; listen to him."
Luminous.meditation: On Mount Tabor, Jesus is transfigured before Peter, James and John. His face shines like the sun. The Father declares: 'This is my beloved Son; listen to Him.' Let us ask for a desire for holiness.

Luminous.title: 5th Luminous Mystery — Institution of the Eucharist
Luminous.verse: Luke 22:19 — "This is my body which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me."
Luminous.meditation: At the Last Supper, Jesus takes bread and wine and says: 'This is my Body... This is my Blood.' He institutes the Eucharist, the sacrament of His abiding presence. Let us ask for a profound love for the Eucharist.

closingText.en:
Eternal God, in whom mercy is endless and the treasury of compassion — inexhaustible, look kindly upon us and increase Your mercy in us, that in difficult moments we might not despair nor become despondent, but with great confidence submit ourselves to Your holy will, which is Love and Mercy itself.

O, Greatly Merciful God, Infinite Goodness, today all mankind calls out from the abyss of its misery to Your mercy — to Your compassion, O God; and it is with its mighty voice of misery that it cries out: Gracious God, do not reject the prayer of this earth's exiles! O Lord, Goodness beyond our understanding, who are acquainted with our misery through and through, and know that by our own power we cannot ascend to You, we implore You, anticipate us with Your grace and keep on increasing Your mercy in us, that we may faithfully do Your holy will all through our life and at death's hour. Amen.
closingText.la: Deus aeterne, in quo misericordia infinita est et thesaurus miserationum inexhaustus, respice in nos benigne et auge misericordiam tuam in nobis, ut in momentis difficilibus non desperaremus neque consternati essemus, sed cum magna fiducia voluntati tuae sanctae — quae est ipsa Caritas et Misericordia — nos submitteremus. Amen.

opening.en:
O God, come to my assistance.
O Lord, make haste to help me.

Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, as it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world without end. Amen.
opening.la:
Deus, in adiutorium meum intende.
Domine, ad adiuvandum me festina.

Gloria Patri, et Filio, et Spiritui Sancto, sicut erat in principio, et nunc, et semper, et in saecula saeculorum. Amen.

intro.en: In honor of the nine choirs of holy Angels and Archangels, we pray the Chaplet of St. Michael. For each choir, pray one Our Father and three Hail Marys.
intro.la: In honorem novem chororum Angelorum et Archangelorum sanctorum, hanc Coronam Sancti Michaelis recitamus. Pro unoquoque choro, unum Pater Noster et tres Ave Maria dicuntur.

choirs.en: By the intercession of St. Michael and the celestial Choir of Seraphim, may the Lord make us worthy to burn with the fire of perfect charity. Amen.
choirs.la: Per intercessionem Sancti Michaelis et caelestem Chorum Seraphim, dignemur Dominus nos inflammare igne perfectae caritatis. Amen.

choirs.en: By the intercession of St. Michael and the celestial Choir of Cherubim, may the Lord grant us the grace to leave the ways of sin and run in the paths of Christian perfection. Amen.
choirs.la: Per intercessionem Sancti Michaelis et caelestem Chorum Cherubim, gratiam nobis Dominus concedat relinquendi vias peccati et currendi in viis christianae perfectionis. Amen.

choirs.en: By the intercession of St. Michael and the celestial Choir of Thrones, may the Lord infuse into our hearts a true and sincere spirit of humility. Amen.
choirs.la: Per intercessionem Sancti Michaelis et caelestem Chorum Thronorum, verum et sincerum humilitatis spiritum in corda nostra Dominus infundat. Amen.

choirs.en: By the intercession of St. Michael and the celestial Choir of Dominations, may the Lord give us grace to govern our senses and overcome any unruly passions. Amen.
choirs.la: Per intercessionem Sancti Michaelis et caelestem Chorum Dominationum, gratiam nobis Dominus largiatur sensus nostros regendi et passiones inordinatas vincendi. Amen.

choirs.en: By the intercession of St. Michael and the celestial Choir of Virtues, may the Lord preserve us from evil and falling into temptation. Amen.
choirs.la: Per intercessionem Sancti Michaelis et caelestem Chorum Virtutum, a malis et a tentationum laqueis nos Dominus conservet. Amen.

choirs.en: By the intercession of St. Michael and the celestial Choir of Powers, may the Lord protect our souls against the snares and temptations of the devil. Amen.
choirs.la: Per intercessionem Sancti Michaelis et caelestem Chorum Potestatum, animas nostras contra insidias et tentationes diaboli Dominus tueatur. Amen.

choirs.en: By the intercession of St. Michael and the celestial Choir of Principalities, may God fill our souls with a true spirit of obedience. Amen.
choirs.la: Per intercessionem Sancti Michaelis et caelestem Chorum Principatuum, verum oboedientiae spiritum animabus nostris Deus infundat. Amen.

choirs.en: By the intercession of St. Michael and the celestial Choir of Archangels, may the Lord give us perseverance in faith and in all good works in order that we may attain the glory of Heaven. Amen.
choirs.la: Per intercessionem Sancti Michaelis et caelestem Chorum Archangelorum, perseverantiam in fide et in omnibus bonis operibus Dominus nobis concedat, ut gloriam caeli consequamur. Amen.

choirs.en: By the intercession of St. Michael and the celestial Choir of Angels, may the Lord grant us to be protected by them in this mortal life and conducted in the life to come to Heaven. Amen.
choirs.la: Per intercessionem Sancti Michaelis et caelestem Chorum Angelorum, in hac vita mortali ab eis protegi et in vita futura in caelum deduci Dominus nobis concedat. Amen.

toMichael.en: O glorious prince St. Michael, chief and commander of the heavenly hosts, guardian of souls, vanquisher of rebel spirits, servant in the house of the Divine King and our admirable conductor — you who shine with excellence and superhuman virtue — deliver us from all evil, who turn to you with confidence and enable us by your gracious protection to serve God more and more faithfully every day.
toMichael.la: O gloriose Princeps Sancte Michael, dux et caput exercituum caelestium, custos animarum, victor spirituum rebellium, minister in domo Regis Divini et admirabilis ductor noster — qui in excellentia et virtute supernaturali fulges — libera nos ab omni malo qui confidenter ad te recurrimus, et da ut tua propitia protectione Deo fidelius quotidie serviamus.

closing.en: Almighty and Everlasting God, Who, by a prodigy of goodness and a merciful desire for the salvation of all men, has appointed the most glorious Archangel St. Michael Prince of Your Church, make us worthy, we ask You, to be delivered from all our enemies, that none of them may harass us at the hour of death, but that we may be conducted by him into Your Presence. Through the merits of Jesus Christ Our Lord. Amen.
closing.la: Omnipotens sempiterne Deus, qui mirabile bonitatis prodigium et misericordes desiderio salutis omnium hominum, gloriosissimum Archangelum Michaelem Principem Ecclesiae tuae constituisti, dignos nos fac, quaesumus, ab omnibus hostibus nostris liberari, ut nulli eorum in hora mortis nostrae molesti sint, sed ab ipso in conspectu tuum perduci mereamur. Per merita Iesu Christi Domini nostri. Amen.

meditation.en: Pilate yields to the cries of the crowd and condemns the innocent Jesus to death. How often do we yield to the crowd and compromise our faith? Lord, grant us courage to stand for truth.
meditation.la: Pilatus clamoribus turbae cedit et Iesum innocentem morti condemnat. Quoties nos quoque turbae cedimus et fidem nostram compromittimus? Domine, da nobis fortitudinem pro veritate standum.

meditation.en: Jesus takes up the heavy Cross without complaint. He carries it willingly out of love for each of us. What cross do you carry today? Offer it to Him.
meditation.la: Iesus Crucem gravem sine querimonia tollit. Eam libenter ex amore erga nos singulos portat. Quam crucem tu hodie portas? Offer eam Ei.

meditation.en: Exhausted and weak, Jesus falls beneath the weight of the Cross. Yet He rises. His fall shows our human fragility; His rising shows divine perseverance. Rise with Him.
meditation.la: Exhaustus et debilis, Iesus sub pondere Crucis cadit. Attamen resurgit. Casus eius fragilitatem humanam ostendit; resurrectio eius perseverantiam divinam. Surge cum Eo.

meditation.en: Mary meets her Son on the way to Calvary. Their eyes meet in unspeakable sorrow and love. Mary stood firm in faith when all others fled. Ask her to strengthen your faith.
meditation.la: Maria Filium suum in via ad Calvariam occurrit. Oculi eorum in dolore et amore ineffabili se contuuntur. Maria in fide stabilis manebat dum ceteri fugerant. Roga eam fidem tuam corroborare.

stations.title: Simon of Cyrene Helps Carry the Cross

meditation.en: Simon is compelled to help Jesus carry the Cross. He who came as a bystander became a sharer in redemption. How do we help carry the cross of our neighbors?
meditation.la: Simon cogitur Iesu Crucem portare adiuvare. Qui ut spectator venerat, particeps redemptionis factus est. Quomodo nos crucem proximorum portare adiuvamus?

stations.title: Veronica Wipes the Face of Jesus

meditation.en: A woman of Jerusalem steps forward and wipes the face of Jesus. An act of tender mercy in the midst of cruelty. On her cloth, His image remains. Do we seek His face?
meditation.la: Mulier Hierosolymitana progreditur et faciem Iesu abstergit. Actus misericordiae tenellae in medio crudelitatis. In velo eius imago eius manet. Nos quoque faciem Eius quaerimus?

meditation.en: Jesus falls again. The weight of our sins and the sins of the world press Him down. Still He rises for love of us. How many times has He lifted us from our own falls?
meditation.la: Iesus iterum cadit. Pondus peccatorum nostrorum et totius mundi eum premit. Et tamen surgit ex amore nostri. Quoties ipse nos a propriis casibus erexit?

stations.title: Jesus Speaks to the Women of Jerusalem

meditation.en: Jesus pauses to console the weeping women: 'Daughters of Jerusalem, weep not for me, but for yourselves and your children.' Even in agony, His heart is for others.
meditation.la: Iesus sistit ut mulieres flentes consoletur: 'Filiae Hierusalem, nolite flere super me, sed super vos ipsas flete et super filios vestros.' Etiam in agonia, cor Eius aliis patet.

meditation.en: Jesus falls a third time, nearly unable to continue. The end is near. He could call ten thousand angels — but does not. Love holds Him to the Cross.
meditation.la: Iesus tertio cadit, vix progredi potens. Finis adest. Posset decem milia angelorum vocare — sed non vocat. Amor eum Cruci affigit.

stations.title: Jesus is Stripped of His Garments

meditation.en: Jesus is stripped of His garments, left with nothing. He is humiliated so that we might be clothed in grace and dignity. He empties Himself so we may be filled.
meditation.la: Iesus vestimentis spoliatur, nihil ei relictum. Humiliatur ut nos gratia et dignitate induamur. Semetipsum exinanit ut nos impleamur.

stations.title: Jesus is Nailed to the Cross

meditation.en: The nails pierce His hands and feet. He is fixed to the Cross. 'Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do.' His first word is forgiveness. For us.
meditation.la: Clavi manus eius et pedes transfigunt. Cruci affigitur. 'Pater, ignosce illis, quia nesciunt quid faciunt.' Primum verbum Eius est venia. Pro nobis.

meditation.en: 'It is finished.' Jesus bows His head and gives up His spirit. The veil of the Temple tears in two. In His death, the door to the Father is opened for all.
meditation.la: 'Consummatum est.' Iesus inclinat caput et spiritum tradit. Velum templi in duas partes scissum est. In morte Eius, ianua ad Patrem omnibus aperta est.

stations.title: Jesus is Taken Down from the Cross

meditation.en: The body of Jesus is laid in the arms of His Mother. Mary holds Him as she did at Bethlehem. From manger to tomb, she was faithful. Entrust yourself to her arms.
meditation.la: Corpus Iesu in ulnas Matris Eius deponitur. Maria eum tenet sicut in Bethleem. A praesepio usque ad sepulchrum, fidelis erat. Te Ipse eius ulnis committe.

stations.title: Jesus is Laid in the Tomb

meditation.en: Jesus is laid in the tomb and the stone is rolled shut. It appears to be the end. But this is not the end — it is three days before the dawn of eternity.
meditation.la: Iesus in sepulchrum ponitur et lapis advolutus est. Finis esse videtur. Sed hic non est finis — tres dies ante auroram aeternitatis.

closing.en: Lord Jesus Christ, Your passion and death is the sacrifice that unites earth and heaven and reconciles all people to You. May we who have faithfully reflected on these mysteries follow in Your steps and so come to share Your glory in heaven where You live and reign with the Father and the Holy Spirit, one God, for ever and ever. Amen.
closing.la: Domine Iesu Christe, passio et mors tua sacrificium est quod terram et caelum unit omnesque homines tibi reconciliat. Da ut nos, qui haec mysteria fideliter meditati sumus, vestigia tua sequentes, gloriam tuam in caelo, ubi cum Patre et Spiritu Sancto, unus Deus, vivis et regnas in saecula saeculorum participes fieri mereamur. Amen.

text.en:
Lord, have mercy on us.
Christ, have mercy on us.
Lord, have mercy on us.
Christ, hear us.
Christ, graciously hear us.

God the Father of heaven, have mercy on us.
God the Son, Redeemer of the world, have mercy on us.
God the Holy Spirit, have mercy on us.
Holy Trinity, one God, have mercy on us.
text.la:
Kyrie, eleison.
Christe, eleison.
Kyrie, eleison.
Christe, audi nos.
Christe, exaudi nos.

Pater de caelis, Deus, miserere nobis.
Fili, Redemptor mundi, Deus, miserere nobis.
Spiritus Sancte, Deus, miserere nobis.
Sancta Trinitas, unus Deus, miserere nobis.

text.en:
Holy Mary, pray for us.
Holy Mother of God, pray for us.
Holy Virgin of virgins, pray for us.
St. Michael, pray for us.
St. Gabriel, pray for us.
St. Raphael, pray for us.
All you holy Angels and Archangels, pray for us.
text.la:
Sancta Maria, ora pro nobis.
Sancta Dei Genitrix, ora pro nobis.
Sancta Virgo virginum, ora pro nobis.
Sancte Michael, ora pro nobis.
Sancte Gabriel, ora pro nobis.
Sancte Raphael, ora pro nobis.
Omnes sancti Angeli et Archangeli, orate pro nobis.

text.en:
St. John the Baptist, pray for us.
St. Joseph, pray for us.
All you holy Patriarchs and Prophets, pray for us.
text.la:
Sancte Ioannes Baptista, ora pro nobis.
Sancte Ioseph, ora pro nobis.
Omnes sancti Patriarchae et Prophetae, orate pro nobis.

text.en:
St. Peter, pray for us.
St. Paul, pray for us.
St. Andrew, pray for us.
St. James, pray for us.
St. John, pray for us.
St. Thomas, pray for us.
St. Mary Magdalene, pray for us.
All you holy Apostles and Evangelists, pray for us.
text.la:
Sancte Petre, ora pro nobis.
Sancte Paule, ora pro nobis.
Sancte Andrea, ora pro nobis.
Sancte Iacobe, ora pro nobis.
Sancte Ioannes, ora pro nobis.
Sancte Thoma, ora pro nobis.
Sancta Maria Magdalena, ora pro nobis.
Omnes sancti Apostoli et Evangelistae, orate pro nobis.

text.en:
St. Stephen, pray for us.
St. Lawrence, pray for us.
St. Agnes, pray for us.
St. Cecilia, pray for us.
St. Perpetua & Felicity, pray for us.
All you holy Martyrs, pray for us.
text.la:
Sancte Stephane, ora pro nobis.
Sancte Laurenti, ora pro nobis.
Sancta Agnes, ora pro nobis.
Sancta Caecilia, ora pro nobis.
Sanctae Perpetua et Felicitas, orate pro nobis.
Omnes sancti Martyres, orate pro nobis.

text.en:
St. Gregory the Great, pray for us.
St. Ambrose, pray for us.
St. Augustine, pray for us.
St. Jerome, pray for us.
St. Thomas Aquinas, pray for us.
All you holy Bishops and Confessors, pray for us.
text.la:
Sancte Gregori, ora pro nobis.
Sancte Ambrosi, ora pro nobis.
Sancte Augustine, ora pro nobis.
Sancte Hieronyme, ora pro nobis.
Sancte Thoma Aquinas, ora pro nobis.
Omnes sancti Episcopi et Confessores, orate pro nobis.

text.en:
St. Anthony of Padua, pray for us.
St. Benedict, pray for us.
St. Dominic, pray for us.
St. Francis of Assisi, pray for us.
St. Ignatius of Loyola, pray for us.
All you holy Priests and Levites, pray for us.
text.la:
Sancte Antoni Patavine, ora pro nobis.
Sancte Benedicte, ora pro nobis.
Sancte Dominice, ora pro nobis.
Sancte Francisce, ora pro nobis.
Sancte Ignati, ora pro nobis.
Omnes sancti Sacerdotes et Levitae, orate pro nobis.

text.en:
St. Anne, pray for us.
St. Monica, pray for us.
St. Teresa of Ávila, pray for us.
St. Thérèse of Lisieux, pray for us.
All you holy men and women, Saints of God, make intercession for us.
text.la:
Sancta Anna, ora pro nobis.
Sancta Monica, ora pro nobis.
Sancta Teresia Abulensis, ora pro nobis.
Sancta Theresia a Iesu Infante, ora pro nobis.
Omnes Sancti et Sanctae Dei, intercedite pro nobis.

text.en:
Be merciful, spare us, O Lord.
Be merciful, graciously hear us, O Lord.

From all evil, deliver us, O Lord.
From all sin, deliver us, O Lord.
From Your wrath, deliver us, O Lord.
From a sudden and unprovided death, deliver us, O Lord.
By the mystery of Your holy Incarnation, deliver us, O Lord.
By Your Cross and Passion, deliver us, O Lord.
By Your death and burial, deliver us, O Lord.
By Your holy Resurrection, deliver us, O Lord.
By Your Ascension into heaven, deliver us, O Lord.
text.la:
Propitius esto, parce nobis, Domine.
Propitius esto, exaudi nos, Domine.

Ab omni malo, libera nos, Domine.
Ab omni peccato, libera nos, Domine.
Ab ira tua, libera nos, Domine.
A subitanea et improvisa morte, libera nos, Domine.
Per mysterium sanctae Incarnationis tuae, libera nos, Domine.
Per Crucem et Passionem tuam, libera nos, Domine.
Per mortem et sepulturam tuam, libera nos, Domine.
Per sanctam Resurrectionem tuam, libera nos, Domine.
Per admirabilem Ascensionem tuam, libera nos, Domine.

closing.en:
Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, spare us, O Lord.
Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, graciously hear us, O Lord.
Lamb of God, who takes away the sins of the world, have mercy on us.

O God, our refuge and our strength, look down with mercy upon Your people. By the intercession of the glorious and immaculate Virgin Mary, Mother of God, of Saint Joseph her spouse, of the blessed Apostles, and of all the saints, in Your mercy hear our prayers. Through Christ Our Lord. Amen.
closing.la:
Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccata mundi, parce nobis, Domine.
Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccata mundi, exaudi nos, Domine.
Agnus Dei, qui tollis peccata mundi, miserere nobis.

Deus, refugium nostrum et virtus, populo tuo misericorditer respice. Per intercessionem gloriosae et immaculatae Virginis Mariae, Genitricis Dei, Sancti Ioseph sponsi eius, beatorum Apostolorum et omnium Sanctorum, precibus nostris in misericordia tua exaudi. Per Christum Dominum nostrum. Amen.

text.en:
Soul of Christ, sanctify me.
Body of Christ, save me.
Blood of Christ, inebriate me.
Water from the side of Christ, wash me.
text.la:
Anima Christi, sanctifica me.
Corpus Christi, salva me.
Sanguis Christi, inebria me.
Aqua lateris Christi, lava me.

text.en:
Passion of Christ, strengthen me.
O Good Jesus, hear me.
Within Thy wounds hide me.
text.la:
Passio Christi, conforta me.
O bone Iesu, exaudi me.
Intra tua vulnera absconde me.

title.en: Defend Me in the Hour of Death

text.en:
Suffer me not to be separated from Thee.
From the malignant enemy, defend me.
At the hour of my death, call me.
text.la:
Ne permittas me separari a te.
Ab hoste maligno defende me.
In hora mortis meae voca me.

text.en:
And bid me come unto Thee,
That with Thy Saints I may praise Thee,
Forever and ever. Amen.
text.la:
Et iube me venire ad te,
Ut cum Sanctis tuis laudem te
In saecula saeculorum. Amen.

text.en:
I arise today
Through a mighty strength, the invocation of the Trinity,
Through belief in the Threeness,
Through confession of the Oneness
Of the Creator of creation.
text.la:
Surgo hodie
Per potentiam virtutis, per invocationem Trinitatis,
Per fidem in Trinitatem,
Per confessionem Unitatis
Creatoris creationis.

text.en:
I arise today
Through the strength of Christ's birth with His baptism,
Through the strength of His crucifixion with His burial,
Through the strength of His resurrection with His ascension,
Through the strength of His descent for the judgment of doom.
text.la:
Surgo hodie
Per virtutem nativitatis Christi cum eius baptismo,
Per virtutem crucifixionis eius cum sepultura,
Per virtutem resurrectionis eius cum ascensione,
Per virtutem descensus eius ad iudicium extremum.

text.en:
I arise today
Through the strength of the love of cherubim,
In the obedience of angels,
In the service of archangels,
In hope of resurrection to meet with reward,
In prayers of patriarchs,
In predictions of prophets,
In preachings of apostles,
In faith of confessors,
In innocence of holy virgins,
In deeds of righteous men.
text.la:
Surgo hodie
Per fortitudinem amoris cherubin,
In oboedientia angelorum,
In servitio archangelorum,
In spe resurrectionis ad praemium,
In precibus patriarcharum,
In vaticiniis prophetarum,
In praedicatione apostolorum,
In fide confessorum,
In innocentia sanctarum virginum,
In iustitia virorum rectorum.

text.en:
I arise today, through
God's strength to pilot me,
God's might to uphold me,
God's wisdom to guide me,
God's eye to look before me,
God's ear to hear me,
God's word to speak for me,
God's hand to guard me,
God's shield to protect me,
God's host to save me
From snares of devils,
From temptation of vices,
From everyone who shall wish me ill.
text.la:
Surgo hodie per
Fortitudinem Dei ut me gubernet,
Potentiam Dei ut me sustineat,
Sapientiam Dei ut me ducat,
Oculum Dei ut ante me prospiciat,
Aurem Dei ut me audiat,
Verbum Dei ut pro me loquatur,
Manum Dei ut me custodiat,
Scutum Dei ut me protegat,
Exercitum Dei ut me salvet
A laqueis daemonum,
A tentatione vitiorum,
Ab omnibus qui mihi mali sint.

text.en:
Christ with me,
Christ before me,
Christ behind me,
Christ in me,
Christ beneath me,
Christ above me,
Christ on my right,
Christ on my left,
Christ when I lie down,
Christ when I sit down,
Christ when I arise.
text.la:
Christus mecum,
Christus ante me,
Christus post me,
Christus in me,
Christus subtus me,
Christus supra me,
Christus a dextris,
Christus a sinistris,
Christus ubi cubo,
Christus ubi assido,
Christus ubi surgo.

text.en:
Christ in the heart of every man who thinks of me,
Christ in the mouth of everyone who speaks of me,
Christ in every eye that sees me,
Christ in every ear that hears me.

I arise today
Through a mighty strength, the invocation of the Trinity,
Through belief in the Threeness,
Through confession of the Oneness
Of the Creator of creation.
text.la:
Christus in corde omnis qui de me cogitat,
Christus in ore omnis qui de me loquitur,
Christus in oculo omnis qui me videt,
Christus in aure omnis qui me audit.

Surgo hodie
Per potentiam virtutis, per invocationem Trinitatis,
Per fidem in Trinitatem,
Per confessionem Unitatis
Creatoris creationis.

closing.en:
Salvation is of the Lord.
Salvation is of the Lord.
Salvation is of Christ.
May Thy salvation, O Lord, be ever with us. Amen.
closing.la:
Salus Domini est.
Salus Domini est.
Salus Christi est.
Salus tua, Domine, semper nobiscum sit. Amen.

sign.en: In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit. Amen.
sign.la: In nomine Patris, et Filii, et Spiritus Sancti. Amen.

ourFather.en:
Our Father, Who art in heaven, hallowed be Thy name; Thy kingdom come; Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven.

Give us this day our daily bread; and forgive us our trespasses as we forgive those who trespass against us; and lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil. Amen.
ourFather.la:
Pater noster, qui es in caelis, sanctificetur nomen tuum. Adveniat regnum tuum. Fiat voluntas tua, sicut in caelo et in terra.

Panem nostrum quotidianum da nobis hodie, et dimitte nobis debita nostra sicut et nos dimittimus debitoribus nostris. Et ne nos inducas in tentationem, sed libera nos a malo. Amen.

crux.en:
Holy cross be my light, let not the dragon be my guide.
Step back Satan, never suggest vain things to me.
Evil are the things you offer, drink your own poison.
crux.la:
Crux sacra sit mihi lux, non draco sit mihi dux.
Vade retro satana, nunquam suade mihi vana.
Sunt mala quae libas, ipse venena bibas.

gloryBe.en: Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit. As it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world without end. Amen.
gloryBe.la: Gloria Patri, et Filio, et Spiritui Sancto. Sicut erat in principio, et nunc, et semper, et in saecula saeculorum. Amen.

begin.en:
On the large beads:
Christ conquers, Christ reigns, Christ commands.

On the 10 small beads:
Holy [Name of Saint], help him/her/them.
begin.la:
On the large beads:
Christus vincit, Christus regnat, Christus imperat.

On the 10 small beads:
Sancte/a [Nomen Sancti], tu illum/illam/eos adiuva.

christus.en: Christ conquers, Christ reigns, Christ commands.
christus.la: Christus vincit, Christus regnat, Christus imperat.

salve.en: Hail, Holy Queen, Mother of Mercy, our life, our sweetness and our hope. To thee do we cry, poor banished children of Eve; to thee do we send up our sighs, mourning and weeping in this valley of tears. Turn then, most gracious Advocate, thine eyes of mercy toward us, and after this our exile, show unto us the blessed fruit of thy womb, Jesus. O clement, O loving, O sweet Virgin Mary!
salve.la: Salve, Regina, mater misericordiae; vita, dulcedo et spes nostra, salve. Ad te clamamus exsules filii Hevae. Ad te suspiramus gementes et flentes in hac lacrimarum valle. Eia ergo, advocata nostra, illos tuos misericordes oculos ad nos converte. Et Iesum, benedictum fructum ventris tui, nobis post hoc exsilium ostende. O clemens, o pia, o dulcis Virgo Maria.

stmichael.en: St. Michael the Archangel, defend us in battle, be our protection against the wickedness and snares of the devil. May God rebuke him we humbly pray; and do thou, O Prince of the Heavenly host, by the power of God, cast into hell Satan and all the evil spirits who prowl about the world seeking the ruin of souls. Amen.
stmichael.la: Sancte Michael Archangele, defende nos in proelio, contra nequitiam et insidias diaboli esto praesidium. Imperet illi Deus, supplices deprecamur: tuque, Princeps militiae caelestis, Satanam aliosque spiritus malignos, qui ad perditionem animarum pervagantur in mundo, divina virtute, in infernum detrude. Amen.

text.en: Glorious St. Michael, guardian and defender of the Church of Jesus Christ, come to the assistance of His followers, against whom the powers of hell are unchained.
text.la: Gloriose Sancte Michael, custos et defensor Ecclesiae Iesu Christi, ades in auxilium sequacium eius, contra quos potestates inferni solutae sunt.

text.en: St. Michael, Angel of Peace, pray for us that the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, may guard our hearts and minds.
text.la: Sancte Michael, Angele Pacis, ora pro nobis ut pax Dei, quae omnem intellectum superat, corda nostra et mentes custodiat.

text.en: O Victorious Prince, most humble guardian of the Church of God and of faithful souls, who with such charity and zeal took part in so many conflicts, defend us.
text.la: O Princeps Victoriosissime, humillime custos Ecclesiae Dei et animarum fidelium, qui tanta caritate et zelo in tot certaminibus participasti, defende nos.

text.en: St. Michael, you who stand by the altar of incense, present my prayers to the Most High.
text.la: Sancte Michael, qui ante altare incensi assistis, preces meas ad Altissimum perfer.

text.en: Valiant warrior, defend us against the attacks of the enemy of our souls.
text.la: Valide bellator, defende nos contra impetus hostis animarum nostrarum.

text.en: Angel of the Last Judgment, grant us the grace to live faithfully according to God's commandments.
text.la: Angele Iudicii Extremi, gratiam nobis concede ut fideliter secundum mandata Dei vivamus.

text.en: Glorious Prince of the heavenly court, guide us in the ways of virtue.
text.la: Gloriose Princeps caelestis aulae, duc nos in viis virtutis.

text.en: St. Michael, support us in our final hour and bring us safely to the judgment seat of Christ.
text.la: Sancte Michael, sustenta nos in hora nostra extrema et ad tribunal Christi nos secure perduc.

text.en: O mighty Archangel, we entrust our lives, our families, and our parish to your vigilant protection.
text.la: O potens Archangele, vitam nostram, familias nostras et paroeciam nostram tutelae tuae vigilanti committimus.

novena_olph.title: Novena to Our Lady of Perpetual Help

text.en: O Mother of Perpetual Help, grant that I may ever invoke thy most powerful name, which is the safeguard of the living and the salvation of the dying.
text.la: O Mater Perpetui Auxilii, da ut nomen tuum potentissimum semper invocare possim, quod est praesidium viventium et salus morientium.

text.en: O Purest Mary, O Sweetest Mary, let thy name henceforth be ever on my lips.
text.la: O Purissima Maria, O Dulcissima Maria, sit nomen tuum ex hoc semper in labiis meis.

text.en: Delay not, O Blessed Lady, to help me whenever I call on thee, for, in all my needs, in all my temptations I shall never cease to call on thee.
text.la: Ne moreris, O Beata Domina, me adiuvare quoties te invoco; nam in omnibus necessitatibus meis, in omnibus tentationibus, te invocare non cessabo.

text.en: O Mother of Perpetual Help, thou art the dispenser of all the goods which God grants to us miserable sinners.
text.la: O Mater Perpetui Auxilii, tu es dispensatrix omnium bonorum quae Deus nobis miserabilibus peccatoribus concedit.

text.en: O Mother of Perpetual Help, we come before thy picture to pour out our hearts to thee.
text.la: O Mater Perpetui Auxilii, ante imaginem tuam venimus ut corda nostra tibi effundamus.

text.en: Thou art the advocate of the most wretched and abandoned sinners who have recourse to thee.
text.la: Tu es advocata miserrimorum et derelictorum peccatorum qui ad te confugiunt.

text.en: O Mother of Perpetual Help, thou art my hope. Help me, dearest Mother, for I recommend myself to thee.
text.la: O Mater Perpetui Auxilii, tu es spes mea. Adiuva me, carissima Mater, quia me tibi commendo.

text.en: In thy hands I place my eternal salvation and to thee do I entrust my soul.
text.la: In manibus tuis pono salutem meam aeternam et tibi animam meam committo.

text.en: O Mother of Perpetual Help, beg of Jesus to grant me the grace to always have recourse to thee.
text.la: O Mater Perpetui Auxilii, roga Iesum ut gratiam mihi concedat semper ad te recurrendi.

text.en: Holy Spirit! Lord of Light! From Your clear celestial height, Your pure beaming radiance give.
text.la: Spiritus Sancte! Domine Luminis! De clara tua caelesti altitudine, puram tuam radiantem claritatem da.

text.en: Come, Father of the poor. Come, treasures which endure; Come, Light of all that live!
text.la: Veni, Pater pauperum. Veni, datorum munerum. Veni, lumen cordium!

text.en: Thou, of all consolers best, Visiting the troubled breast, Dost refreshing peace bestow.
text.la: Consolator optime, dulcis hospes animae, dulce refrigerium.

text.en: Thou in toil art comfort sweet, Pleasant coolness in the heat, Solace in the midst of woe.
text.la: In labore requies, in aestu temperies, in fletu solatium.

text.en: Light immortal! Light Divine! Visit Thou these hearts of Thine, And our inmost being fill!
text.la: O lux beatissima, reple cordis intima tuorum fidelium.

text.en: If Thou take Thy grace away, Nothing pure in man will stay, All his good is turn'd to ill.
text.la: Sine tua divinitate, nihil est in homine, nihil est innoxium.

text.en: Heal our wounds; our strength renew; On our dryness pour Thy dew; Wash the stains of guilt away.
text.la: Lava quod est sordidum, riga quod est aridum, sana quod est saucium.

text.en: Bend the stubborn heart and will; Melt the frozen, warm the chill; Guide the steps that go astray.
text.la: Flecte quod est rigidum, fove quod est frigidum, rege quod est devium.

text.en: Thou, on those who evermore Thee confess and Thee Adore, In Thy sevenfold gifts, Descend. Amen.
text.la: Da tuis fidelibus, in te confidentibus, sacrum septenarium. Da virtutis meritum, da salutis exitum, da perenne gaudium. Amen.

text.en: Most holy Apostle, St. Jude, faithful servant and friend of Jesus, the Church honors and invokes you universally, as the patron of difficult cases, of things almost despaired of.
text.la: Sanctissime Apostole Sancte Iuda, serve fidelis et amice Iesu, Ecclesia te universaliter honorat et invocat, ut patronum causarum difficilium, rerum prope desperatarum.

text.en: Pray for me, I am so helpless and alone. Intercede with God for me that He bring visible and speedy help where help is almost despaired of.
text.la: Ora pro me, tam impotente et solitario. Intercede apud Deum pro me ut auxilum visibile et celere adducat ubi auxilium prope desperatum est.

text.en: Come to my assistance in this great need that I may receive the consolation and help of heaven in all my necessities, tribulations, and sufferings.
text.la: Adsis mihi in hac magna necessitate ut consolationem et auxilium caeleste in omnibus necessitatibus, tribulationibus et doloribus meis accipere possim.

text.en: I promise you, O Blessed St. Jude, to be ever mindful of this great favor granted me by God.
text.la: Promitto tibi, O Beate Sancte Iuda, me semper memorem fore huius magni beneficii a Deo mihi concessi.

text.en: I promise to always honor you as my special and powerful patron, and to gratefully encourage devotion to you.
text.la: Promitto me semper te honoraturum ut patronum meum specialem et potentem, ac gratanter devotionem erga te fovere.

text.en: May the Most Sacred Heart of Jesus be adored, and loved in all the tabernacles until the end of time.
text.la: Adoretur et ametur Sacratissimum Cor Iesu in omnibus tabernaculis usque ad consummationem saeculi.

text.en: May the most Holy Heart of Jesus be praised and glorified now and forever.
text.la: Laudetur et glorificetur sanctissimum Cor Iesu nunc et in saecula saeculorum.

text.en: St. Jude, pray for us and hear our prayers. St. Jude, maker of miracles, pray for us.
text.la: Sancte Iuda, ora pro nobis et exaudi preces nostras. Sancte Iuda, miraculorum effector, ora pro nobis.

text.en: St. Jude, helper of the hopeless, pray for us. Amen.
text.la: Sancte Iuda, auxiliator desperatorum, ora pro nobis. Amen.

text.en: O glorious St. Augustine, the light and oracle of the faithful, I most fervently join with thee in returning thanks to God for the endless blessings bestowed on thee.
text.la: O gloriose Sancte Augustine, lux et oraculum fidelium, ferventissime tecum Deo gratias ago pro infinitis beneficiis tibi collatis.

text.en: Thou who wast once a captive of error and a slave to thy passions, obtained by the tears of thy holy mother, St. Monica, the grace of a sincere conversion.
text.la: Tu qui quondam captivus erroris et servus passionum, per lacrimas sanctae matris tuae, Sanctae Monicae, gratiam sincerae conversionis obtinuisti.

text.en: O holy Doctor of the Church, pray that I may imitate thy profound humility and detest my past transgressions.
text.la: O sancte Doctor Ecclesiae, ora ut profundam humilitatem tuam imiter et praeterita delicta mea detestor.

text.en: Obtain for me the grace of true contrition and a firm resolution to amend my life.
text.la: Obtine mihi gratiam verae contritionis et firmum propositum vitam meam emendandi.

text.en: Intercede for me, that I may be consumed with the fire of divine love which so wholly possessed thy soul.
text.la: Intercede pro me, ut igne amoris divini consumar qui animam tuam tam plene possedit.

text.en: St. Augustine, guide my intellect and will, that I may clearly understand and firmly embrace the truths of our holy faith.
text.la: Sancte Augustine, intellectum et voluntatem meam dirige, ut veritates sanctae fidei nostrae clare intellegam et firmiter amplectar.

text.en: O great penitent, help me to remain watchful over my senses and passions.
text.la: O magne paenitens, adiuva me vigilantem manere super sensus et passiones meas.

text.en: Grant that, imitating thy example in life, I may, through thy intercession, reach the eternal joy of heaven.
text.la: Concede ut, exemplo tuo in vita imitato, per intercessionem tuam, aeternam caeli laetitiam consequar.

text.en: O God, who didst wonderfully convert St. Augustine, grant us the grace to serve Thee with pure hearts and minds. Amen.
text.la: O Deus, qui Sanctum Augustinum mirabiliter convertisti, concede nobis gratiam tibi corde et mente puro serviendi. Amen.

text.en: O my Jesus, You have said: 'Truly I say to you, ask and you will receive, seek and you will find, knock and it will be opened to you.' Behold I knock, I seek and ask for the grace of...
text.la: O mi Iesu, dixisti: 'Amen dico vobis, petite et dabitur vobis, quaerite et invenietis, pulsate et aperietur vobis.' Ecce pulso, quaero et peto gratiam...

text.en: Sacred Heart of Jesus, I place all my trust in You.
text.la: Cor Sacratissimum Iesu, in Te omnem fiduciam meam pono.

text.en: O my Jesus, You have said: 'Truly I say to you, if you ask anything of the Father in My name, He will give it to you.' Behold, in Your name, I ask the Father for the grace of...
text.la: O mi Iesu, dixisti: 'Amen dico vobis, si quid petieritis Patrem in nomine meo, dabit vobis.' Ecce, in nomine tuo, Patrem peto gratiam...

text.en: Sacred Heart of Jesus, I place all my trust in You.
text.la: Cor Sacratissimum Iesu, in Te omnem fiduciam meam pono.

text.en: O my Jesus, You have said: 'Truly I say to you, heaven and earth will pass away, but My words will not pass away.' Encouraged by Your infallible words I now ask for the grace of...
text.la: O mi Iesu, dixisti: 'Amen dico vobis, caelum et terra transibunt, verba autem mea non transibunt.' Verbis tuis infallibilibus confirmatus, nunc peto gratiam...

text.en: Sacred Heart of Jesus, I place all my trust in You.
text.la: Cor Sacratissimum Iesu, in Te omnem fiduciam meam pono.

text.en: O Sacred Heart of Jesus, for whom it is impossible not to have compassion on the afflicted, have pity on us miserable sinners.
text.la: O Cor Sacratissimum Iesu, cui impossibile est non misereri afflictorum, miserere nobis miserabilibus peccatoribus.

text.en: Grant us the grace which we ask of You, through the Sorrowful and Immaculate Heart of Mary, Your tender Mother and ours.
text.la: Concede nobis gratiam quam a Te petimus, per Cor Dolorosum et Immaculatum Mariae, Matris tuae et nostrae.

text.en: Hail Holy Queen... St. Joseph, foster father of Jesus, pray for us. Amen.
text.la: Salve Regina... Sancte Ioseph, nutricie Iesu, ora pro nobis. Amen.
